evidence.voice_certainty: "The fact is, the membership was never real" is flat and direct, but "That single account does not prove everyone's experience" reintroduces conspicuous editorial hedging.
evidence.method_execution: "If BAD SUGAR is something you freely choose ... why are you reading this book?" becomes the trap question, followed by credit reassignment and the inversion of loss into escape.
evidence.structure_anatomy: The chapter previews and escalates through named sections to an all-caps belief verdict, the assigned second instruction, and a SUMMARY, though its opening thesis is not italicized and intermediate verdict peaks are sparse.
evidence.repetition_mantra: MN-03, "the sugar trap," receives an argue-then-name debut and recurs verbatim; MN-01 is echoed exactly in both the body and SUMMARY.
evidence.emotional_register: "You are not losing a membership. You are dropping a forgery" sustains escape, but the chapter never supplies the reference's full-force danger beat before disowning fear.
evidence.rhythm_texture: The turnip questions and "No. Something that durable must be well hidden" create Carr-like snap, while the club, con, and investment explanations run too symmetrically and too long.

gap_summary: The candidate reproduces the belief-trap machinery but replaces the reference's blunt, escalating factual nerve with polished, qualified reassurance and extended abstraction.

suggestions.suggestion: At "That single account does not prove everyone's experience" and "though it may," stop narrating evidentiary caution inside the persuasion: retain only a genuinely required qualification, then follow the example with a flat settled-fact verdict.

Before "A NAMED TRAP — AND A REASON TO BE CHEERFUL," assign one compact, evidence-backed high-stakes consequence beat, then explicitly tell the reader to put fear behind them; this restores the reference's wound-then-bandage nerve instead of moving straight from analogy to reassurance.

The club, confidence-trick, and fraudulent-investment passages all prove the same point; cut the second full hypothetical, keep one concrete scene and one trap question, and compress the proof into a two-line verdict so the rhythm peaks instead of circling.

Turn the long opening sentence into the italic thesis and add a short all-caps compression after the first proof beat, so the preview-to-argument-to-verdict ladder is visible well before the final third.

The "2ND INSTRUCTION" headline packs the action, threshold, clinical exception, and stopped-reader branch into one long block; make the numbered headline one cheerful imperative and move its conditions into the prose immediately below.
